ogTitle: Four Corners vs Fowlers Gap: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Four Corners and Fowlers Gap.
heading: Four Corners vs Fowlers Gap — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Four Corners, New South Wales and Fowlers Gap, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Four Corners has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Fowlers Gap (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Four Corners or Fowlers Gap safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Four Corners has a flood risk score of 48/100, while Fowlers Gap scores 28/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Four Corners scores 21/100 for bushfire risk. Fowlers Gap scores 54/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Four Corners or Fowlers Gap?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Four Corners has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.37% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
